queue.task: Afternoon direction: prioritize test gaps vs prepared statements vs OOM fix
queue.summary: Prioritized: test gaps first (fast ROI), then prepared statements. OOM deferred. Queue unchanged.

queue.goal: monkey-lang WASM test coverage: do-while, for-in, inline map/filter
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: inline HOF tests (map/filter/reduce/find/any/every) + edge cases for do-while/for-in. Tests exist but inline optimization path untested.

queue.task: Add inline HOF builtin tests (map/filter/reduce/find/any/every) to wasm-compiler.test.js
queue.summary: Added 14 inline HOF optimization tests: inline map (5), inline filter (4), inline/runtime equivalence (2), chained inline (3). 1961/1961 tests pass.

queue.task: Add edge case tests for do-while and for-in WASM compilation
queue.summary: Added 13 edge case tests: do-while (5: single iter, nested, break, closure, array mutation) + for-in (8: empty, single, nested, break, range, closure creation, mutation, strings). 1974/1974 pass.
Git push both repos. Both clean. monkey-lang 1974 tests, HenryDB 4327 tests.
Planned 2 BUILD tasks: (1) avoid deep clone in prepared stmt by using AST param slots, (2) batch execute + ? placeholders. db.prepare() already exists but deep-clones on every exec.

queue.task: Optimize prepared stmt execution: avoid deep clone, add paramSlots to AST
queue.summary: Optimized prepared stmts: fast bind/unbind replaces deep clone. 2.7x faster (0.043→0.016ms/insert). collectParamSlots pre-walks AST, fastBind swaps in-place, fastUnbind restores. All 64 prepared stmt tests pass.

queue.task: Add batch execute (stmt.executeMany) and ? placeholder support
queue.summary: Added ? placeholders (auto-numbered positional params) and stmt.executeMany() for bulk inserts. executeMany 2.6x faster than individual execute(). 64/64 prepared stmt tests pass.
Git push all repos. All clean.

queue.task: Assess HenryDB OOM at 5K rows — profile memory usage with indexes
queue.summary: OOM at 5K not reproducible with current code (50K rows OK at 66MB). Root cause was likely old deep-clone in prepare. Found 3 remaining O(N) heap scans in INSERT: (1) ON CONFLICT upsert, (2) composite unique check, (3) RETURNING — materializes entire heap per insert. RETURNING is worst offender, should return values from _insertRow directly.

queue.goal: Fix HenryDB OOM at 5K rows + redundant heap scan in INSERT
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: (1) RETURNING fix — use rid from _insertRow to read back values O(1), (2) ON CONFLICT upsert — use PK index for conflict detection O(log N).

queue.task: Fix RETURNING: use rid from _insertRow instead of heap scan
queue.summary: Fixed INSERT RETURNING: O(1) rid lookup replaces O(N) heap scan. New _readRowByRid uses heap.get(pageId,slotIdx). Works for normal and ON CONFLICT RETURNING. 0.052ms/insert at 2K rows. All tests pass.

queue.task: Fix ON CONFLICT upsert: use PK index instead of heap scan
queue.summary: Fixed ON CONFLICT upsert: O(log N) PK index lookup replaces O(N) heap scan. Falls back to scan for unindexed columns. 0.046ms/upsert at 5K rows. All tests pass.
Git push all repos. Both clean.

queue.task: Depth check: what surprised me in the last 5 BUILD tasks?
queue.summary: Depth check passed (3/3): RETURNING heap scan was surprising O(N) perf bug, learned in-place AST mutation pattern for prepared stmts, found 2 algorithmic issues. No breadth spiral.

queue.goal: monkey-lang: lazy runtime function emission for WASM
queue.summary: Planned 1 BUILD task: replace _addRuntimeFunctions() with _requireRuntime() lazy pattern. 49 runtime funcs registered upfront; many programs use <10. Approach: on-demand emission during compilation.

queue.task: Implement lazy runtime function emission: _requireRuntime() adds functions on first use
queue.summary: CANCELLED: Lazy runtime emission not worth it. Compile time is already 0.22-0.28ms. Savings would be ~0.1ms (30%). Found interesting false alarm: passing AST to compile() instead of string caused Lexer to tokenize [object Object], creating infinite loop. Not a compiler bug. Updated TODO to remove this item.

queue.task: What else in monkey-lang deserves exploration? Review WASM compiler for untested edge cases

queue.goal: HenryDB: add defensive guard for compile(ast) vs compile(string) misuse

queue.task: Add input type guard to WasmCompiler.compile() for non-string inputs

queue.goal: monkey-lang: stress test WASM compiler with complex programs
